with respect to notice, the DEM
ocratic platform contains no rec
ommendation. It's only ineligible
declaration in regard to injunction
suits is the reiteration of the plank
I in the platforms of 1896 and 1904
providing that, in prosecutions for
contempt in federal courts where
violation of the order constituting
contempt charged is outside the
presence of the court there shall
be a jury trial.


1.-THE extended operation of such
provision to weaken the power
of the courts in the enforcement
Of lawful orders can hardly be
over-stated. The administration of
justice lies at the foundation of the
government. The maintenance of
the authority of the courts is es-
sential unless we are prepared to
embrace anarchy. Never in the
history of the country has there
been such an insiduous attack upon
the judicial system as the proposal
to interject jury trial between all
orders of the courts made after a
full hearing and the enforcement
of such orders"


In discussing interstate regula-
lions Taft said:


when employees of an interstate
railway enter upon peaceful
strike to secure better wages, in
common law this Is not restraint of
trade or violation of the rights of
the company or the public. 1t
ought not be made violation OF
the antitrust law.

THE democratic platform rec
ommends tax on national banks
and such state banks as may come
In, in the nature of enforced in
surance to raise the guaranty fund
to pay depositors of any bank
which fails. The proposition is to
tax the honest, prudent banker to
make up for the dishonesty and
imprudency of others. If the pro-
posal were adopted exactly as the
democratic platform suggests 1t
would bring the whole banking
system of the country down in
ruins"
